summary: Small assorted decompilation-matching PR that added matches for mnGallery_80258940, grDisplay_801C5F60, and grMaterial_801C8E28, renamed symbol fn_8025896C to mnGallery_8025896C, created basic mnGallery declarations/header scaffolding, and made a minor inline-expression cleanup in baselib jobj.h.
what_changed: Added grDisplay_801C5F60, which obtains a camera object with GET_COBJ(gobj), calls HSD_CObjSetCurrent, sets gobj->gxlink_prios to 8, calls Camera_800310A0(0), calls HSD_GObj_80390ED0(gobj, 7), and ends the current camera with HSD_CObjEndCurrent.
Added grMaterial_801C8E28 as a small wrapper around it_802756D0(gobj).
Declared grMaterial_801C8E28 in src/melee/gr/grmaterial.h with the address comment /* 1C8E28 */.
Added mnGallery_80258940, which allocates 0x271000 bytes with HSD_MemAlloc and stores the result in extern void* mnGallery_804D6C8C.
Added a new mnGallery header guard, platform include, baselib/gobj.h include, and prototype for mnGallery_80258940.
Renamed config symbol fn_8025896C to mnGallery_8025896C, preserving address .text:0x8025896C and size 0x9C.
Changed HSD_JObjSetupMatrix inline condition from jobj == NULL to !jobj while preserving the same null-or-not-dirty early return logic.
smart_moves: Matched very small leaf/wrapper functions by expressing their true high-level behavior directly, such as grMaterial_801C8E28 forwarding to it_802756D0.
For grDisplay_801C5F60, used existing baselib camera/object helpers rather than open-coding state changes, which keeps the decomp readable and aligned with project conventions.
Introduced mnGallery.h while adding the first concrete mnGallery function, giving future gallery decompilation work a proper place for prototypes.
Updated symbols.txt from a generic fn_ name to a subsystem-prefixed mnGallery_ name once the function’s module identity was clear.
The jobj.h change used !jobj, likely as a matching/style-sensitive expression simplification without changing behavior.
decomp_lessons: When a previously empty module receives its first matched function, add the module header and minimal prototypes immediately so later work has a stable declaration point.
Small wrapper functions are valuable matches: even a one-call forwarding function such as grMaterial_801C8E28 can remove an unmatched symbol and clarify call graph intent.
Symbol names should follow the owning subsystem prefix once evidence allows it; this PR changed fn_8025896C to mnGallery_8025896C in the symbol map.

For object/camera rendering callbacks, preserve the high-level helper sequence around HSD_CObjSetCurrent and HSD_CObjEndCurrent; this appears to be the idiomatic matching shape for grDisplay_801C5F60.
Minor source spelling can matter for matching or style: !jobj may be preferable to jobj == NULL in inline baselib helpers when it produces the desired output.
naming_conventions: Unidentified functions use fn_ADDRESS until associated with a module, then become module_ADDRESS, as shown by fn_8025896C becoming mnGallery_8025896C.
Melee subsystem symbols use camel-like module prefixes with original address suffixes, for example mnGallery_80258940, grDisplay_801C5F60, and grMaterial_801C8E28.
Header guard added for mnGallery used the lowercase underscore style _mngallery_h_.
Address comments in headers follow the compact form /* 1C8E28 */ before declarations.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use direct wrapper implementations for functions that only call an existing helper, demonstrated by grMaterial_801C8E28 calling it_802756D0.
Preserve exact constants visible from the match: mnGallery_80258940 allocates 0x271000 bytes; grDisplay_801C5F60 assigns gxlink_prios = 8 and calls HSD_GObj_80390ED0 with 7.
Use existing macros/helpers like GET_COBJ, HSD_CObjSetCurrent, HSD_CObjEndCurrent, and HSD_MemAlloc to reach the expected code shape while keeping source idiomatic.
Boolean/null expression form may be adjusted for matching, as in changing jobj == NULL to !jobj inside HSD_JObjSetupMatrix.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or formal review feedback were present in the provided PR slice.
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that implement mnGallery_8025896C after the symbol rename in PR1450.
Search for other grDisplay camera callback matches using HSD_CObjSetCurrent and HSD_CObjEndCurrent.
Search for additional grMaterial wrappers around it_802756D0 or it_802756E0.
Check whether mngallery.h later gains additional prototypes and whether the _mngallery_h_ guard style remains consistent.
